key_insight: The paper accelerates phase-field cohesive zone modeling to simulate mixed-mode fracture and high-cycle fatigue of UHPC and HSS with experimental fidelity.
engineering_relevance: This enables more practical durability assessment of high-performance infrastructure materials under cyclic loading without exhaustive cycle-by-cycle testing.
method: Derive a generic stress-based failure criterion for UHPC from biaxial tensile tests.
Embed a fatigue degradation function into the PF-CZM fracture framework.
Use an envelope-load acceleration algorithm with adaptive cyclic increments to capture the three-stage fatigue process efficiently.
evidence_signal: The model is validated against relevant experimental comparisons and is reported to capture both mixed-mode fracture and high-cycle fatigue behavior of UHPC and HSS.
watch_item: Accuracy depends on the chosen failure criterion, fatigue degradation law, and envelope-load acceleration assumptions, which may need recalibration for other materials or loading histories.
paper_title: High performance construction materials fracture and high cycle fatigue assessment based on accelerated PF-CZM
